title: Deserialization methods should be provided for OptionalField members
description: Deserialization methods should be provided for "OptionalField" membersAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugserializationWhy is this an issue?Fields marked withSystem.Runtime.Serialization.OptionalFieldAttributeare serialized just like any other field. But such fields are ignored on deserialization, and retain the default values associated with their types. Therefore, deserialization event handlers should be declared to set such fields during the deserialization process.This rule raises when at least one field with theSystem.Runtime.Serialization.OptionalFieldAttributeattribute is declared but one (or both) of the following event handlersSystem.Runtime.Serialization.OnDeserializingAttributeorSystem.Runtime.Serialization.OnDeserializedAttributeare not present.Noncompliant cod
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Classes with [Serializable] attribute and at least one [OptionalField] attribute but missing both [OnDeserializing] and [OnDeserialized] handlers
logic.triggering_conditions: Has [Serializable] AND has [OptionalField] AND (missing [OnDeserializing] AND missing [OnDeserialized])
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude test classes, generated code, and classes with at least one deserialization handler present
logic.reasoning: OptionalField members retain default values during deserialization unless explicitly set in OnDeserializing or OnDeserialized handlers. This rule detects classes that declare optional fields but lack the necessary deserialization event handlers to initialize them. We check for Serializable + OptionalField presence, then verify absence of both handler types. Uses custom function for complex attribute checking across class source code since AST doesn't provide separate attribute nodes with names.
